provider_name: LaGuardia Community College – Adult & Continuing Education
item_name: LaGuardia Community College – Adult & Continuing Education

findings.question: Does the program have published fees?
findings.note: The captured text does not contain any explicit details about the published fees for courses at LaGuardia Community College's Adult & Continuing Education program. While the page mentions various programs and services, it does not give specific fee information or confirm its existence directly.
findings.short_note: Fee information is not present on the page.

findings.question: Is the program a noncredit-bearing transition program? (must be YES)
findings.note: The page provides information about continuing education at LaGuardia Community College but does not specify whether the transition program is noncredit-bearing or credit-bearing. The absence of this clarification means the criterion cannot be confidently verified from the text provided.
findings.short_note: Page does not specify if the program is noncredit-bearing.

findings.question: Does the coursework address skill-building and employment outcomes?
findings.note: The page suggests that Continuing Education at LaGuardia Community College includes elements like job training, career advancement, and growing a business, which implies employment-related courses. However, explicit details about courses specifically addressing skill-building and employment outcomes are not provided. This requires further review for clarification. [The model's quoted snippet could not be verified against the captured page content, so it was discarded and this finding was held at Needs Review.]
findings.short_note: The page is ambiguous on skill-building and employment outcomes.

findings.question: Is the published course fee within the program's fee caps (≤$350/course or ≤$800/month)?
findings.note: Form-stated fee ($300) is within the $350 cap. This form states a per-course fee, so the $350/course cap is applied. If the program instead bills monthly, the $800/month cap applies instead — a reviewer should confirm the billing basis. [Held at Needs Review: the supporting text was found but a readable, targeted screenshot of the exact proof could not be produced, so a reviewer should confirm this directly on the site.]
findings.short_note: Needs Review — proof found in text but not capturable as a targeted screenshot.

findings.question: Are all staff/volunteers/trainers screened for criminal background & excluded-provider status? (letter required)
findings.note: Requires a screening-confirmation letter from the program — this cannot be inferred from the provider's public website.

findings.question: Is the Transition program currently approved in the budget?
findings.note: Requires the participant's approved Self-Direction budget record showing Transition Program as an approved spending category.

findings.question: Has the individual already completed their educational program?
findings.note: Transition programs are for adults who have aged out of their prior educational program — confirm from the participant's record, not the provider's website.

findings.question: Does the coursework address the individual's valued outcome?
findings.note: Requires the participant's Life Plan valued-outcome narrative and reviewer judgment.

findings.question: Is the program provided in an OPWDD-certified location? (must be NO)
findings.note: Requires cross-referencing the agency's internal list of OPWDD-certified locations, which isn't public data.

findings.question: Can the program be funded by ACCES-VR, IDEA, or any other source? (must be NO)
findings.note: Requires knowledge of the participant's ACCES-VR/IDEA/other funding eligibility — not determinable from the provider's website.
